title: HP: The Best Alternative to Mainframe Computing — How to Get There From Here
author: Gustavo Fernandez (Hewlett-Packard) at MFASC'92 (Mainframe Alternative Solution Conference '92, HP Brazil)
abstract: Hewlett-Packard's MFASC'92 (Mainframe Alternative Solution Conference '92) deck by Gustavo Fernandez — 'Shattering the Glass House / HP: The Best Alternative to Mainframe Computing'. Pitches the technology shift (>10x performance growth, 16-way MP), top IS issues (alignment, BPR, information architecture, data utilization), and detailed five-year cost-justification scenarios comparing IBM-mainframe maintain vs HP-migration paths. Cites Forrester Research and 'The P.A. Report' as third-party validation. Vendor parallel/foil to Aberdeen's 1992-1993 mainframe-alternative thesis.
importance_rationale: Vendor-side artifact of the same 1992-1993 mainframe-alternative wave Aberdeen and Kastner were chronicling; HP Brazil hosted the conference, signaling international scope.
relevance_rationale: Parallel-track vendor positioning to Aberdeen's IS/downsizing research; useful for triangulating analyst vs vendor framing in the 1992-1993 era.
prescience_rationale: Five-year TCO modeling (lease + maintenance + facilities + operations) anticipated TCO methodology that became Gartner's marquee analytical product through the 1990s; 'glass house' metaphor and 16-way MP roadmap captured the mainframe-vs-midrange transition that played out 1993-2000.
